filer: U.S. Senator Edward J. Markey et al.
summary: A senators' letter urging FERC to act so households are not forced to bear data-center-driven costs, citing record PJM capacity prices and tens of billions in rate increases.
key_points: Data-center and cryptomining demand is driving residential bills up.
Cites PJM's record $16.6B capacity auction and ~$71B in 2025 rate hikes.
Demands FERC act to protect ratepayers from cost-shifting.

quotes.text: We write to urge the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to take immediate action to ensure that U.S. households are not forced to bear the costs of the growing nationwide demand for energy by data centers.
